Program

The dialysis program aims to train technicians who can provide pre-treatment, intra-treatment and post-treatment care to patients who are planned for dialysis treatment as a result of renal failure.

Our students carry out the relevant applications in the

dialysis laboratory

in order to contribute to their knowledge before clinical practice in accordance with the content of the theoretical courses accompanied by expert lecturers.

Dialysis Laboratory Equipment and Studies

It is a laboratory with a capacity of 3 hemodialysis devices arranged for the students of our university SHMYO Dialysis Program to practice the theoretical trainings they have seen in the courses for 4 semesters starting from the 1st grade.

Device and Material Type

Fresenius 4008 S Classix Hemodialysis Machine

Fresenius 5008 S Hemodialysis Machine

Fresenius Artery-Venous Needle

Fresenius Dialysis Solution

Bicarbonate Solution Bibag

All equipment used for invasive procedures

In the Dialysis Laboratory,

our students learn all the systems necessary to connect a dialysis patient to a hemodialysis machine and follow up.

Recognize hemodialysis machine equipment

Learning hemodialysis machine alarm systems

Hemodialysis machine preparation (placing the equipment in the machine before the session and removing it from the machine at the end of the session)

Preparing the hemodialysis patient vascular access for hemodialysis procedure

Medicine preparation

Qualifications of Dialysis Laboratory Technicians

Candidates who want to be a dialysis technician;

Who loves their profession, people and can empathize,

Friendly and tolerant

Dedicated, patient and meticulous

Able to work in accordance with ethical rules,

Prone to teamwork,

Interest in mechanical devices,

Careful and investigative,

To follow the developments in the field of profession, current publications, scientific publications,

Work with blood and urine samples in close contact with the patient

must be individuals.

In addition, dialysis technician candidates should be sensitive to the use of dialysis machines and the alarm it will give, and they should not have any discomfort, disability, disease that restricts the movements of the person and should not have any obstacles to use their ears, eyes and hands.
